B–6 For each point P in B, let Sp be the set of points with all coordinates equal to ±1 which differ from P in ex- actly one coordinate. Since there are more than 2n+1 /n points in B, and each Sp has n elements, the cardinali- ties of the sets Sp add up to more than 2n+1, which is to say, more than twice the total number of points. By the pigeonhole principle, there must be a point in three of the sets, say Sp, SQ, SR. But then any two of P, Q, R dif- fer in exactly two coordinates, so PQR is an equilateral triangle, as desired.
